title: need solution to deal with command which need both single and double quote combined with pipe or output redirection
body:
**Description**

I've  already read [Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/troubleshooting.md#3-buildah-run-command-fails-with-pipe-or-output-redirection) about the solution to "buildah run command fails with pipe or output redirection"

take this as an example:  https://github.com/docker-library/php/blob/703a3d0a4e4c149bfd62fc3e7b71645f9496b178/7.4/alpine3.11/fpm/Dockerfile#L174

```bash
runDeps="$( \
	scanelf --needed --nobanner --format '%n#p' --recursive /usr/local \
		| tr ',' '\n' \
		| sort -u \
		| awk 'system("[ -e /usr/local/lib/" $1 " ]") == 0 { next } { print "so:" $1 }' \
)"; \
apk add --no-cache $runDeps
```

 it's hard to do with buildah run when the command need use both single and double quote,
because `sh -c` itself need single quote

any suggestions please ?  how to `buildah run` the command above conveniently?
 
**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
no need to


**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**


**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.12.0-2.fc31.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.12.0
Go Version:      go1.13.5
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.1-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.0.0
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 08:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
Fedora release 31 (Thirty One)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux localhost.localdomain 5.4.15-200.fc31.x86_64 #1 SMP Tue Jan 28 09:08:32 UTC 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body:
@ttys3 I've tried playing with it a bunch this afternoon evening and have run out of steam.  I know what's happening, but haven't found the adjustment yet.  The problem is the `$(scanelf ...)` isn't running inside of the container, but within the host.  If you use the `--` option, I thought it would then do everything within the container, but again it's still expanding and running everything in the parens.  For instance:

```
# buildah run tom  --  runDeps=$(scanelf)
-bash: scanelf: command not found
```
Where as this works:

```
buildah run tom -- /bin/sh -c scanelf
* Scan ELF binaries for stuff
{lots of help info removed for brevity}
```

So my thinking is you could potentially do something like:
```
 buildah run tom -- /bin/sh -c scanelf --needed --nobanner --format '%n#p' --recursive /usr/local | tr ',' '\n' | sort -u | awk 'system("[ -e /usr/local/lib/" $1 " ]") == 0 { next } { print "so:" $1 }'
```

and possibly capture that output and feed that to `apk add` command?

```
bulidah copy tom tom.txt /
buildah run tom -- /bin/sh -c apk add --no-cache  /tom.txt
## or
buildah run tom -- /bin/sh -c apk add --no-cache $(cat tom.txt)
```

But that's not working, doesn't look like apk know what to do with the file, it returns apk help.  I'm not an apk heavy, any thoughts?
This is not an issue with buildah, but is a scripting issue.  I would advise you to put your advanced scripting into a shell script and add it to the container image.
